bibliographic_data.title: Evolution of Electronic Structure in Atomically Thin Sheets of WS2 and WSe2
bibliographic_data.abstract: Geometrical confinement effect in exfoliated sheets of layered materials leads to significant evolution of energy dispersion with decreasing layer thickness. Molybdenum disulphide (MoS2) was recently found to exhibit indirect to direct gap transition when the thickness is reduced to a single monolayer, leading to remarkable enhancement in photoluminescence efficiency. Here we report differential reflectance and photoluminescence spectra of mono- to few-layer WS2 and WSe2 that indicate similar indirect to direct transition when thinned to a single monolayer. Strong enhancement in PL quantum yield is observed for mono-layer WS2 and WSe2 due to exciton recombination at the direct band edge.

core_content.novelty_claim: The study reports the evolution of optical absorption and photoluminescence spectra in atomically thin sheets of WS2 and WSe2, showing a similar indirect to direct transition as observed in MoS2.
core_content.key_methodology: Mechanically exfoliated flakes of WS2 and WSe2 were deposited on quartz or SiO2/Si substrates. Differential reflectance spectra were measured to determine the absorbance changes with decreasing layer thickness.
outcomes: Indirect to direct transition in band structure observed for WS2 and WSe2 monolayers
Strong enhancement in photoluminescence quantum yield for mono-layer WS2 and WSe2
core_content.full_text_markdown: The study investigates the evolution of electronic structure in atomically thin sheets of WS2 and WSe2, focusing on their optical properties. The researchers found that these materials exhibit an indirect to direct band gap transition when thinned to a single monolayer, similar to MoS2. Differential reflectance spectra were used to measure the absorbance changes with decreasing layer thickness, revealing gradual blueshift of excitonic absorption bands. Photoluminescence (PL) measurements showed strong enhancement in quantum yield for mono-layer WS2 and WSe2 due to exciton recombination at the direct band edge.
